summary.intent: Monitor and maintain a Codex AI review loop on PR #724 in fogodev/ars-ui, fixing any CI failures that block the review from completing.
summary.outcome: A CI linker crash (Bus error in rust-lld) affecting the adapter-test job was diagnosed as a resource exhaustion issue and fixed by serializing Cargo build concurrency for that workflow job; the fix was committed and pushed as commit on the PR branch.

repo: The adapter-test CI job compiles all adapter and harness test targets simultaneously with all features, which can exhaust memory on GitHub hosted runners during linking of large Leptos/Dioxus test binaries.
The project uses `cargo xtest adapter` as the contract for running adapter tests, orchestrated via xtask.
CI workflow lives at .github/workflows/ci.yml; xtask orchestration in xtask/src/test.rs and xtask/src/ci/mod.rs.
Codex review is triggered via PR comments (`@codex review`) and tracked by the 👀 reaction on the PR.

code.finding: Adapter Tests job definition; serialized Cargo build jobs added here to prevent concurrent linking of large test binaries.
Adapter test orchestration logic; consulted to understand what `cargo xtest adapter` compiles.
Additional test configuration referenced during CI failure diagnosis.
CI module logic consulted to understand build job scoping before making the workflow change.

workflow: A `Bus error` from rust-lld in CI without any test assertion failure indicates a linker OOM/resource issue, not a code bug — the fix is concurrency reduction, not code changes.
Polling cadence of 120s sleep between Codex reaction/thread/CI checks is the established review loop rhythm for this project.
Commits to this repo require a `Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>` trailer.
The skill file at .agents/skills/waiting-for-codex-review/SKILL.md defines the Codex review polling protocol used in this session.

friction: The commit hook took multiple polling cycles to complete before the push could proceed.
GitHub check runs take time to attach after a push, requiring idle waiting before CI status is visible.
The adapter-test linker crash (Bus error in rust-lld) was a flaky-looking failure that required log inspection to distinguish from a real test regression.
Two separate CI fix cycles were needed: first a Dioxus SSR fix (prior to this transcript), then the linker concurrency fix.
open_items: Codex review was still in progress (👀 reaction, no unresolved threads) at end of session — review outcome not yet known.

The serialized Cargo build workaround is a mitigation, not a root fix; a more targeted solution (e.g. splitting the adapter matrix, using a larger runner, or splitting test compilation from execution) could be revisited.
It is unknown whether the new CI run triggered after the linker fix will pass all checks cleanly.
